title: Beloperone riedeliana
taxonomy: Beloperone riedeliana is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to Mexico. The classification of Beloperone riedeliana is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, the clade Eudicots, the clade Asterids, the order Lamiales, the family Acanthaceae, the tribe Acantheae, and the genus Beloperone. The species is further classified into the binomial nomenclature Beloperone riedeliana. This classification helps in identifying and organizing the plant's taxonomy.

intro: Beloperone riedeliana, also known as Beloperone, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Beloperone riedeliana include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
